item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES! I JUST IMAGINED THAT! EDWARD WAFFLEHANDS!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a waffle iron that turns things into waffles. This would probably be at home in the thing-that-does-a-thing environment of Series I, but with how little narrative meat is here it doesn't fit into the site today.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also what would happen if you were to put another waffle maker into it?
And would the second waffle maker be able to make waffles still?

item.title: Wait... it doesn't need to be solid?
item.content:encoded: Now that I know that materials that are put in don't have to be solid, what would happen if you were to just close the waffle maker (the scp) with just air in there, or helium, or any other gas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the problem here is a failure of imagination;
it makes anything put into it waffle shaped, without structurally damaging it.
ANYTHING.

Tungsten Carbide is an extraordinarily hard and resilient metal alloy with an extraordinarily high melting point. It can only be worked using industrial-grade forging and casting machinery. The typical approach is to treat it like fictional adamantium- a material that cannot be altered once cool- you cast it by melting powdered tungsten and carbon at thousands of degrees C, polish it with diamond powder, and then use it for machining puny earth metals like carbon steel.

This cast iron waffle iron could make a tungsten carbide waffle using 1.3 newtons of force and the power from a standard wall outlet. And it would be cool to the touch when opened.

Other impossible things it could presumably make:
functioning cellphone waffles
bonzai tree and planter waffles
Diamond Waffles
Rock waffles. Not re-hardened lava waffles. The same rock you put in, but now waffle-shaped.
Book waffles.
Intact egg waffles
Sand Waffles
Water Waffles (it never once says the matter has to be solid.)

This thing, in short, is not merely an anomaly. It is an abomination unto physics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh this suuuucks +1 for reasons I can't articulate

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You get indestructible waffle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the article but

Regardless of the material so placed

I can't help but be slightly irked at the phrasing
Maybe it's just me???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused. Could you please explain to me how an appliance that creates very much not dead, waffle-shaped mice is non-anomalous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it made anything put into it into a WAFFLE, as in actual edible waffle, it would be anomalous. But as described, all it does is basically cook anything put into it, the end result coming out looking like a waffle solely due to the construction of the cooker being designed to cook waffles.

It's like cartoons where they put hands in a wafflemaker and it comes out with pound-sign soot on it. It's played straight in this article, and that's it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's certainly anomalous. Displace any material to form a waffle. Want to make a tungsten waffle? Go right ahead.

Now, I can see why you might find this boring, but you're clearly entirely and objectively wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried selecting the empty space below the article for a hidden message of some kind but there was none. Is there something missing to justify the huge amount of upvotes this completely non-anomalous thing has?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you put one of the many indestructible SCP's in this and close it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well if the initial execution and style of writing is met and enough of the community deemed it worth keeping well it can. I personally found the image intriguing and the content fascinating and now I want waffles for breakfast with honey and butter so +1

As long as no one turns my possessions into waffles of course

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, everyone upvoted a machine, that turns everything into waffles, because it's funny? Well, I don't agree with you. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pfft, I +1'd and I'm not sorry at all.

I regret nothinnnnng.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't know why, but I found this upvotable. +1'd Why? I'm so sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would have worked as an anomalous item, but doesn't seem to be a good addition to the mainlist. Sorry, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Talk about setting a bad example for new readers, I fucking hate this. +1
So bad it's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is so stupid and clearly does not belong on this site.

+1 Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE WAFFLES ARE RISING AGAINST US!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmm. It's not that I don't see where you're coming from, I mean, the object itself is, as you said, ridiculous. It's a direct import of a bog-standard cartoon prop. If it had been written with the kind of care and respect that such a thing deserves in this context, the article would have been just one more unremarkable piece of downvote bait.

I guess what hooked me was that it wasn't. Instead, the article constructed around this not-the-good-kind-of-silly artifact is exactly the kind of thing I hope to read when I come here. The writing is clinical, detached, spare almost to the point of obfuscation, and it left me with a mental image of someone stuck with the job of quantifying yet another in a seemingly endless series of arbitrarily bizarre objects that stopped being interesting (to the quantifier) a long time ago.

Part of it is also the fact that the author started off with such a clown-shoe of a subject item and still managed to pull off something that feels like an end-note in a research paper. I see lots of people try that, but it's harder than it looks. *shrug*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I thought this was utterly ridiculous. It's a waffle maker what doesn't kill you immediately. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes and no. You get a waffle-shaped lump of water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last part was the horrific part, huh? Every reader must have read that and imagined what would happen if they put their hand in there.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This object brings new meaning to the phrase, "Make me a waffle."

Because now, we CAN!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if we put a block of ice in it? Does the ice get melted instead of enwaffled?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a brazen, shameless cartoon waffle iron.

This waffle iron is a lone wolf loose cannon with no respect for authority who'll do whatever it takes to get the job done.

This waffle iron is exactly what this town needs.

+1.

Take that, The Man!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wh… okay, why the hell am I laughing at this. Why.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rare and beautiful waffle-rat… I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to take this seriously and say that this could kill 682, but if we could control 682 enough to shove it into a waffle iron, we wouldn't need to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Iron Waffle is now my headcanon name for a breakfast-themed cooking competition show…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frisbees? Nah. Good drain-covers though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I couldn't eat an iron waffle? Darn.
Well, at least it'd make good frisbees for those times when you lose them.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully weird. I wonder how the Foundation found out about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682 WAFFLES FOR EVERYONE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I don't get a goddamn test log in the next 24 hours someone's getting stabbed in the eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting for great justice.

Question: If you make a Pufferkitten waffle, does it still have toxic levels of d'aaaaaaaw?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THANK 343 WE FINALLY HAVE A WAFFLE MAKER

EDIT: Upon consulting my idea list, I can now toss my own idea for one, which was kind of similar, but inferior. (Any batter cooked in it would turn into a mass of flesh in the shape of a waffle.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think what I like about this the fact that the changes to the matter are entirely topological. It is basically straight out of a cartoon. It is a Tom & Jerry waffle iron, complete with your hand coming out looking like a waffle but otherwise still being a hand. Except instead of blowing into your thumb to reinflate your hand, the terrible thing that is reality ensues, and your hand dies slowly and painfully because hands are not meant to look that way.

item.title: Re: Maybe not
item.content:encoded:
It was the Inception joke that pushed you over the edge, huh.

I deserve this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. Now this… this is something right here.

My headcanon now states that this was intended to be part of a set with the cannibal's banquet, the cheese liquid, the jello-fountain, and the salad-making SCP i'm going to write this weekend.

item.title: Re: Maybe not
item.content:encoded:
Nyeeeah… so I was in a good mood when I read this first and the enthusiasm was contagious. This does amuse me a great deal, but it really is just another OTT item in the end. The only horror aspect of it is that it can turn part of someone's body into a waffle shape and… well… so what? How often do you stick your body parts in a waffle iron?

As much as I don't want the old cliche list to influence new pieces it's just not something to stand up for the ages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WAFFLECEPTION

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: make a waffle, then put it in this, then put the result back in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this mean…. you can make Waffles a waffle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't blame me, blame whoever created tacnayn in response to nyan cat…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling this one. It seems too much like an OOT object and it doesn't really have a good hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quit picking on cats…but dump the neighbor's chihuahua in it, by all means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And for some reason my first thought is what a cat would look like after a run-in with this thing. If I upvote will you get that image out of my head?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this shouldn't be so good but it is great holy shit

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've said it at least three times, and I'll say it again.

What.

+1

item.title: Upvoted
item.content:encoded: OTT WAFFLEMAKER PLS SEE CLICHE LIST FOR WHY THIS IS WRONG GET OFF THIS WIKI YOU NUB

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Waffles for the waffle god! Waffles for the waffle god! WAFFLES FOR THE WAFFLE GOD! (and syrup for the syrup throne!)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is definitely one of those "This shouldn't be good, but it is" entries. And I love those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really thought this would be bad, but for some reason I love it… must be the thought of waffles…

item.content:encoded: Because that's stupid.
